explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **linguistic expressions of providing, supplying, or offering something** (such as giving support, delivering resources, granting access, or furnishing assistance).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse domains including international relations, commercial agreements, infrastructure projects, healthcare aid, legal directives, and political support. It is multilingual—present in English, Arabic, Italian, Spanish, French, Romanian, Turkish, Swedish, and others—and is often found in formal, transactional, or descriptive contexts involving institutional or interpersonal exchange.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: High-activation texts consistently contain verbs or phrases denoting the act of provision (e.g., “provide,” “supply,” “give,” “offer,” “support,” “provision of,” “hand over,” “furnish”). Low-activation examples are mostly neutral statements, headlines, or factual descriptions without any semantic frame of giving, supplying, or assisting.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects the semantic role of provision in a sentence, focusing on predicates and constructions that indicate transfer or allocation of resources, services, or support from one party to another. It abstracts away from specific topics or languages to activate on the underlying concept of supplying something.

results.actual_text: detailed his meetings with Russian government officials and others, and said that they provided him with insights and outreach that he was interested in sharing with the campaign,” Schiff said in
results.plain_text: WASHINGTON — Former Trump campaign volunteer Carter Page, who has come under scrutiny in the investigation of Russian election interference, told a House committee that he sought permission for a July 2016 trip to Moscow from senior Trump campaign officials, and reported to other Trump officials about the trip when he returned.  It’s long been known that Page traveled to Moscow in July 2016, but he has said it was in his private capacity, unrelated to his role with the Trump campaign.  Page, whose sworn testimony was released Monday night, told the House Intelligence Committee last week that he sought permission to make the trip from campaign manager Corey Lewandowski, and also notified Hope Hicks, who is now the White House communications director.  Lewandowski told Page he was clear to go on the trip as long as the travel was not associated with his work on the campaign, Page told the committee.  Page also acknowledged that he had been aware that another volunteer campaign adviser, George Papadopoulos, had been meeting with a professor with links to the Kremlin, according to the committee's ranking Democrat, Rep. Adam Schiff, D-Calif.  Papadopoulos has pleaded guilty to lying to the FBI and is cooperating with Special Counsel Robert Mueller.  Both men served on a campaign foreign policy advisory committee under the supervision of then-Sen. Jeff Sessions, who is now the attorney general. Page testified he told Sessions about the July 2016 Moscow trip, it has been previously reported.  Sessions "advised nothing" when Page told him about his plans to travel to Russia, Page said in the transcript.  "Page — after being presented with an email he sent to his campaign supervisors, and which he did not disclose to the committee prior to the interview and despite a subpoena from the Committee — detailed his meetings with Russian government officials and others, and said that they provided him with insights and outreach that he was interested in sharing with the campaign,” Schiff said in a statement.  In terse and sometimes heated exchanges with members of the committee, Page admitted that he had met with Russian officials and discussed the U.S. presidential election “in general terms.” He also met with the head of investor relations at Rosneft, a top Russian oil company. Page said the sanctions the U.S. has imposed on Russia may have come up in their discussion but “not directly.”  Page told the committee he wrote to Mueller on Oct. 5, explaining that he intends to plead the fifth amendment and keep documents related to his work in Russia to himself.  As has been previously reported, Page acknowledged that he may with met with Russian Deputy Prime Minister Arkady Dvorkovich during the July 2016 trip.  In an email on July 8, Page told JD Gordon, a Trump campaign official, that he received “some incredible insights” from his meetings with :Russian legislators and a few members of the presidential administration,” according to the testimony.  “During many public appearances prior to his November 2, 2017 testimony before the House Intelligence Committee in open session, Carter Page characterized his July 2016 trip to Russia as a private one in which his interactions with Russian individuals were largely confined to the 'man on the street,’” Schiff said. "In his testimony, however, he was forced to acknowledge that he communicated with high level Russian officials while in Moscow, including one of Russia’s Deputy Prime Ministers. He also admitted notifying the fact of his meetings to his campaign supervisors.  Page told NBC News Monday night that some lawmakers are focusing on "irrelevant distractions." Last month he told NBC News, "I'm cooperating with everyone in D.C. who might want my assistance." He called the Russia investigation a "witch hunt that was sparked by the dodgy dossier in the months prior to November 2016."  Page has been open about some of his contacts with Russian officials during and before the 2016 presidential campaign, including with former Russian ambassador Sergei Kislyak.  "I'm not going to deny that I talked with him," Page said in March on MSNBC's "All In With Chris Hayes."  In his testimony, Page acknowledged meeting with numerous senior individuals from Russian energy companies Rosneft and Gazprom in Moscow in July and December, according to an NBC News review of the transcript.

predictions.text: “Page — after being presented with an email he sent to his campaign supervisors, and which he did not disclose to the committee prior to the interview and despite a subpoena from the Committee — detailed his meetings with Russian government officials and others, and said that they provided him with insights and outreach that he was interested in sharing with the campaign,” Schiff said in a statement.
predictions.reasoning: This section contains the verb 'provided' and the phrase 'sharing with the campaign,' which directly match the neuron's focus on acts of provision.

results.actual_text: Birthday boy Prayut gives Duterte an early birthday treat BANGKOK, Thailand – Thailand Prime Minister Pray
results.plain_text: Birthday boy Prayut gives Duterte an early birthday treat  BANGKOK, Thailand – Thailand Prime Minister Prayut Chan-o-cha celebrated his 63rd birthday on Tuesday, March 21, but on that day, he also prepared for the birthday of another leader: Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte.  Duterte, who turns 72 on March 28, shared with the Filipino community in Thailand about Prayut's early birthday surprise for him.  "Alam mo kagabi, kabait ng Prime Minister ninyo. Alam mo, birthday niya kagabi, pero alam niya birthday ko rin, malapit na. Alam mo, kaya pala tumindig, 'Sandali lang ah.' Nagpagawa ng cake para sa akin: 'happy birthday,'" recalled Duterte.  (You know, last night, your Prime Minister was so nice. You know, it was his birthday but he also knew my birthday is coming soon. You know, that's why he said, "Just a moment." He had a cake baked for me: "happy birthday.")  The cake-giving supposedly happened during the official dinner Prayut held in Duterte's honor on Tuesday night at the Government House in Bangkok.  During Duterte's toast at the official dinner, he spoke of the friendship between the Philippines and Thailand.  "As Thailand continues to its own onward advance, rest assured that the Philippines will be there as a friend and as a brother to support. As I have said earlier, we are a faithful friend, sir," he said.  Birthday song  Upon hearing Duterte was going to celebrate his birthday soon, the roughly 2,000 Filipinos listening to his speech erupted in song.    After hearing their rendition of "Happy Birthday to You," Duterte quipped: "Salamat po. So 'yung pinakamaganda diyan at walang atraso, puwede tumindig, makapag beso-beso lang naman."  (Thank you. So let the most beautiful person here who does not have any issues with me stand up so we can kiss cheek to cheek.)  His meeting with Thailand's Filipino community was the last event of his 3-day official visit, capping off his tour of Southeast Asian nations.  His visits to all 10 countries of the Assocation of Southeast Asian Nations were intended to help him fulfill his duties as host as the Philippines chairs the regional bloc this year. – Rappler.com

Alam mo, birthday niya kagabi, pero alam niya birthday ko rin, malapit na. Alam mo, kaya pala tumindig, 'Sandali lang ah.' Nagpagawa ng cake para sa akin: 'happy birthday,'' recalled Duterte.

results.actual_text: of 18.5 points per game, Jalen Bridges stepped up and provided 18 points for WVU in Austin. That equals the junior forward’s
results.plain_text: MORGANTOWN, W.Va. — A large crowd awaits the Mountaineers and Longhorns when they meet at 2 p.m. Saturday at the WVU Coliseum. Tickets for the contest, which is a True Blue game, are sold out. Those in attendance will hear a special violin rendition of the National Anthem performed by Master Gunnery Sergeant Peter Wilson, a 30-year veteran of the United States Marine Corps and a 1986 Morgantown High graduate who provided music to five U.S. Presidents from 1990-2020 as a member of “The President’s Own” United States Marine Band. After Sergeant Wilson puts his bow away, West Virginia and Texas will meet on the basketball court for the second time this season. UT took a 74-59 victory from the Mountaineers in Austin on Jan. 1, though WVU was without two key pieces in that contest as both Taz Sherman and Gabe Osabuohien were unable to make the trip after testing positive for COVID. “We didn’t have our full team down there, which had a lot to do with it,” explained WVU head coach Bob Huggins of the New Year’s Day loss at the Erwin Center, where West Virginia holds a 3-7 all-time record. The Mountaineers are 5-4 at the Coliseum against the 'Horns. “If we play as hard as we did the other night (in an 84-81 loss at Iowa State), we’ll be OK. We just have to be able to finish games. “We’ll have our chances.” Without Sherman, who is currently second in the Big 12 in scoring with an average of 18.5 points per game, Jalen Bridges stepped up and provided 18 points for WVU in Austin. That equals the junior forward’s second-best performance of the season, though it wasn’t enough to overcome the strong start by the Longhorns, who raced out to a 39-20 first-half lead and never looked back. Texas, which is currently ranked 20th in the country and holds a 20-8 overall record and a 9-6 mark in the Big 12, was led in its previous win over WVU by senior guard Marcus Carr, who had 20 points. Carr, who played at Minnesota last year and currently averages 11.0 points a game for the 'Horns, is one of six transfers on coach Chris Beard’s roster this season. Among those half-dozen transfers is the team’s overall leading scorer and rebounder in 6-foot-6 senior Timmy Allen, who is averaging 11.9 points and 6.4 rebounds per game. The younger brother of former Mountaineer Teddy Allen, Timmy scored 1,323 points in his three previous seasons at Utah before transferring to Texas prior to the 2021-22 campaign. “They are a lot more cohesive now, I think, playing this long together,” said WVU’s coach of the Longhorns, who have spent the past 28 games blending returnees like Andrew Jones (11.2 ppg) and Courtney Ramey (9.2 ppg) with their many new additions. “Just playing together for this amount of time, I think they’re going to get that way.” * * * * * * West Virginia’s starting point guard, Kedrian Johnson, did not play Wednesday night at Iowa State as he was suffering the effects of bruised hips. His status for Saturday is still questionable, as Huggins said there’s no quick cure for Johnson’s aches and pains. “He’s hurting, and he’s been hurting for a long time,” explained WVU’s coach. “When you’re that skinny and boney without much padding and consistently run into back screens and flare screens and on-ball screens — on-ball screens are the ones that really get you — you get sore. He’s sore and can hardly walk.” * * * * * * Though the Mountaineers have lost 12 of their past 13 games and hold a 14-14 overall record and a 3-12 league mark with three regular-season games to go, Huggins said he’s going to continue to put what he believes is his best lineup on the floor. “I’ve never done anything in my life that I haven’t tried to win at … in my life. From basketball to academics to the myriad of things my father threw at me, I’ve always tried to win. I don’t know anything else.” Huggins still hasn’t given up on taking that competitive spirit into the postseason, even though realistically West Virginia’s NCAA hopes now rest exclusively with winning the Big 12’s automatic berth by capturing the league tournament (March 9-12 in Kansas City). The NCAA Tournament isn’t the only postseason opportunity, though, as there are also the NIT, CBI and CIT. “I’d play all summer if we could,” said WVU’s coach. “I think that’s how you get better. That’s why you call rookies rookies. They’ve never experienced what the older guys have experienced.”

results.plain_text: The latest trade, Performances and Moving Averages give us the following Picture  The share price of Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc (NASDAQ:AGAE) dipped -15.87% to close Friday’s market session at $1.06, lower as compared to yesterday’s close. The stock price fluctuated between $1.0001 and $1.28 throughout the trading session with the volume trading being 215235 shares, which represented a significant variation when compared to the three months average volume of 59.33K shares. The firm’s stock price fluctuated -22.06% within the last five trades and 16.48% within the last 30 trades, which was a significant change from the beginning of this year. Despite the fact that the share price increased 0.95% in the last 6 months and 16.74% was added to its value over the previous 3 months. AGAE stock is trading at a margin of -10.52%, 7.53% and 5.98% apart from the 20-Day, 50-Day and 200-Day Simple Moving Average prices.  As of the close of trading, AGAE deals in the Communication Services domain. The stock is trading -29.33 percent below its 52-week high and 39.47 percent above its 52-week low. For example, looking both at the price and the high and low measurements of 52 weeks will give you a clearer picture of the direction the price is heading. The firm’s Weighted Alpha is -17.3. A positive weighted alpha indicates the firm has done well over the course of the year, whereas one below 0 indicates that the firm has done poorly. What Does Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc’s Profitability and Valuation Ratios Tell Us About the Stock?  With regard to the profitability of the company, the operating margin is currently at -107.66 percent and the profit margin is -61.54 percent, and the company has reported a gross margin of 8.47 percent. The profit margin, also known as the revenue ratio or gross profit ratio, is an efficiency figure used to estimate the business’s profitability by comparing net income and sales. The higher the number, the more profits are generated for the company and vice versa. The stock’s market cap achieved a total value of $39.05 million as of the last trading session. Market capitalization is the total value of all outstanding shares of a corporation and it is used to measure a company’s market value. Forward price-to-earnings is calculated using predicted earnings for the next financial year’s P/E determination. The stock has achieved an effective Price-to-Sales Ratio of 5.74 that mirrors the cost to be found for sales by the market. The firm managed a Price-to-Book ratio of 0.47, which equates the market value of a stock with its book value.  Is Insider Trading a Real Thing?  Almost all investors and traders prefer to invest in shares controlled by the management of a corporation as a management company will be more likely to run the business itself and to never conduct things against the management’s desires and will always try to do what is best for their shareholders. Currently, 69.17 percent of Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc shares are owned by insiders, and 3.12 percent are held by financial institutions. Choi Roy, the 10% Owner at Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc (AGAE) has bought 153,532 shares of firm on Dec 19 at a price of $1.43 against the total amount of $0.22 million. In another inside trade, Choi Roy, 10% Owner of Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc (NASDAQ:AGAE) bought 10,002 shares of the firm on Dec 18 for a total worth of $13503.0 at a price of $1.35. An inside trade which took place on Dec 15, 10% Owner of Allied Gaming & Entertainment Inc Knighted Pastures LLC bought 1,400 shares of firm against total price of $1890.0 at the cost of $1.35 per share.

results.actual_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the
results.plain_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the years, but Thursdays have a storied history of their own. Author Michael Hurd will take part in a live virtual event for the Allen Public Library to discuss the largely unknown history of Black high school football in the state. Hurd’s Thursday Night Lights: The Story of Black High School Football in Texas was named one of the 30 best Texas books for the 2010-19 decade by The Texas Observer and one of the 30 most essential books about Texas by the Houston Chronicle. The live event will be at 7:30 p.m. May 13 at allentx.swagit.com. Former Dallas Morning News columnist James Ragland will moderate the program. Former Dallas Cowboys receiver Tony Hill will also take part. Thursday Night Lights tells the inspiring story of Black high school football in Texas from 1920 to 1970. The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as “Mean” Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles “Bubba” Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career. Hurd is also the author of Black College Football, 1892-1992, the only book that comprehensively documents the legacies of football programs at historically Black colleges, and he serves on the selection committee for the Black College Football Hall of Fame. He is a board member for the Writers’ League of Texas, a trustee with the Friends of the Texas Historical Commission and a member of the Texas Institute of Letters. For details about the virtual event, call 214-509-4911.

The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as 'Mean' Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles 'Bubba' Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career.
